expect.caption: Figure A.7 The left plot shows the synthetic classification data set with data from the two classes shown in red and blue. expect.caption: Figure E.1 A geometrical picture of the technique of Lagrange multipliers in which we seek to maximize a function $f ( \mathbf { x } )$ , subject to the constraint $g ( \mathbf { x } ) = 0$ . If x is D dimensional, the constraint $g ( \mathbf { x } ) = 0$ corresponds to a subspace of dimensionality $D - 1$ , indicated by the red curve. The problem can be solved by optimizing the Lagrangian function $L ( \mathbf { x } , \lambda ) = f ( \mathbf { x } ) \dot { + } \lambda g ( \mathbf { x } )$ .

expect.caption: Figure E.2 A simple example of the use of Lagrange multipliers in which the aim is to maximize $f ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } ) ~ =$ $1 - x _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } - x _ { 2 } ^ { 2 }$ subject to the constraint $g ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } ) = 0$ where $g ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } ) = x _ { 1 } + x _ { 2 } - 1$ . The circles show contours of the function $f ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } )$ , and the diagonal line shows the constraint surface $g ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } ) = \bar { 0 }$ .
